zones.zone_name: Ascent of the Awakened: Audience with the Guardian

encounters.current_strategy_md:
## Mechanics

The primary danger posed by this Dragon is **Breath of Fire**, which spawns 2 flame blobs for every player or pet within the Area of Effect! This will instantly overwhelm a raid!

## Positioning & Pull

Place everyone well out of AE range, pull the Dragon and the tank must get him nose into a crook near an alcove. Now it is a long slow burn.

- **MT's healers** must joust in between the **Breath of Flame** attacks to heal, then get OUT of AE range before the **50 seconds** expires.
- As soon as the 2 mobs (for the MT) spawn, **ST** runs in, grabs them, and drags them well out of range and, assisted by all the melee'rs, kills them.
- **DPS** can assist a little, but all the long-range DPS (**mages** and **bowmen**) should be on the Dragon.
- Use all hate reducers to make sure the **MT** does not lose agro or it is all over!

zones.current_overview_md: You've arrived too late! The orcs of the [Deathfist Empire](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Deathfist_Orcs_(Faction)) have already beaten Kaljanar and take her ring of teleportation to [Deathfist Citadel](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Deathfist_Citadel). Make haste!

encounters.current_strategy_md: - Hail Amulius, and continue speaking with him to get the option to enter Bloodskull Valley: Maulic's Stronghold.
Leader of [The Bloodskull Orcs](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Category:The_Bloodskull_Orcs_(Faction)).

zones.zone_name: Drayek's Chamber: Throne of the Kromise King

encounters.current_strategy_md:
## Positioning

It is recommended that you pull Darathar into the water, and have casters positioned at maximum range to avoid incurable trauma damage.

## Self-Heals & Stuns

He will cast a raid-wide **stun** and heals for **25% of his maximum health** three times during the fight, at **75%**, **50%** and **25%** health. This means:

- At **75%** health, he will heal to full health.
- At **50%** he heals to **75%** health.
- At **25%** he heals back to **50%** health.

After that point, he will no longer heal himself.

## Flame of Ages

His special attack, [Flame of Ages](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Flame_of_Ages), is an **elemental area-of-effect** spell which does a high amount of direct damage and has a damage-over-time component. He casts this approximately every **50 seconds**.

> Each member should have **elemental cure potions** to ensure they can cure themselves in case a healer is unable to cure them immediately.

## Per-Instance Heal Behaviour

The self-heals only occur **once per instance** at each health threshold, not per encounter. For example, if you get Darathar below **50%** and then the raid wipes, Darathar will only heal once during subsequent fights. If the raid wipes after taking Darathar to **25%** health, he will not heal and stun again in that instance.

- Loot her corpse for the [Essence Shard of Soul](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Essence_Shard_of_Soul) needed for [Fire and Ice](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Fire_and_Ice).
- Clear the beach of all other mobs before engaging Mjolni, as all remaining will immediately aggro when Mjolni is pulled.
'The Epitaph of Grandius Lakado' will not drop if Brius Lakado is grey.
- Gives access to the raid instance The Court of Al'Afaz.

zones.zone_name: The Eternal Gorge: A Daring Confrontation
zones.current_overview_md:
## Prep & Initial Trash

Get ready at the top at zone in (buffs, etc). Everyone go downstairs but stay inside tower, have the tank step outside and pull mobs in until the area around the tower is clear. Avoid using out-of-encounter AEs (blue background, etc) because they can hit mobs wandering around the tower and add.

## Progression

Once the tower area is clear, start pulling each mausoleum. There is a named in each with a couple guards. These named will memwipe and aggro a certain class frequently, calling out the name of the person they are going to attack. See map for the type of class each sister will aggro on memwiping. Healers include power healing, so enchanters should watch out on two mobs.

Once all 4 sisters are killed, the "shield" around the Mother is gone and she can be attacked.

## Notes

> The zone does not lock until you kill the Mother — if she bugs out and becomes non-attackable after repeated wipes, you can reset.

encounters.current_strategy_md:
## Strategy 1

### Pull

- **Pet pull** Vyemm to the hallway all the way back at the bottom of the stairs (a rather long pull).
- The raid group should wait behind the door at the top of the stairs to avoid the **frontal AOE** on pull.
- **Healers** should wait at the bottom of the stairs.
- The **MT** waits in the area at the bottom of the stairs to taunt Vyemm while Alzid is distracted. (The goal is to get Alzid to reset after the summoner has run up the stairs, but keep Vyemm with you.)

### Positioning

- Turn Vyemm to be **perpendicular to the hallway**.
- Once properly turned, send in the forces to rush his side.
- Everyone needs to fight from his **belly**. He will move you around during the fight, so stay with him and don't get in front or behind him.

## Strategy 2

### Pull

- Have the raid sit at the top of the side stairs going to the upper level.
- Have a **monk** disband and **FD pull** him to the encounter room. When he resets he will be single pull able. This eliminates the **Heals from the Dracon**.
- Have everyone move down into the room and stand on one side of the entrance against the wall leading into the arena.

### Positioning

- **Tank** will pull him down the stairs and turn him till his head is facing towards the arena and his back legs and tail are in the room.
- Everyone will attack him from the **sides**.
- **Tank healers** will need to fight him under his belly.
- **Dirges and backup healers** can joust or rotate in on the tank if they are hit with aoe.
His treasure chest will drop right in the middle of him. Just right-click his corpse and select **'loot'** to remove his corpse from in-game and make his loot accessible.

This is possibly the hardest hitting named in Veeshan's Peak. It is recommended that you have at least **3 good heals** in the Main Tank group. **Healers** should try to get **Shield of Rainbow Hues** to block Druushk's periodic **Fear AE**.

## Pull & Positioning

- Have your **Main Tank** pull this mob toward the **wall behind him**.
- Have the **MT group** stay tight as **LoS issues** will come up from time to time.
- Rest of the raid will be assisting the **Off Tank**.

## Adds

- At around **75%** or so, a message will pop up warning you that a mob will spawn — a **heroic ^^^ add** which has a pretty nasty AE (**hits harder than the main boss**).
- Have your **OT group** meet the add and be careful that they turn it **away from the raid** or else it means a wipe.
- At this time you will notice that a **targetable but non-killable baby dragon** will be running to groups of eggs. This mob will spawn three more adds (down arrows), but they still can wipe you fairly quickly.

## Rotation

Once those are taken care of, get back on named until the next message about an add incoming. Repeat until dead.

## Positioning

- Everybody except the **tank** stays on the little island that is circling round Hoshkar's "main" isle. You are protected there by an **aura**.
- The **tank** stays on the main isle kiting Hoshkar in range of the little moving island.

## Notes

- If Hoshkar goes in the **lava** he **stoneskins**.
- **Pets** are fine in this fight.

## Pull

Kluzen is the **easiest** of the named monsters in [Veeshan's Peak](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Veeshan's_Peak). Before the fight, make sure to clear all of the nearby trash mobs, as they are roamers — don't forget the [Drakota](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Drakota) flying overhead.

## Mechanics

Kluzen is practically a **straight burn**. He does a large AOE called **wingbeat** (**45 seconds**) that must be **announced and jousted**, but that is all.

## Overview

After the engineer goes down for the first time, **one decent healer can keep one decent MT alive** vs. the Named.

## Pull

- **MT** pulls the named anywhere convenient.
- **Raid assists the MA.** On pull, MA should target a **"generator"** that has appeared somewhere around the circle that the named is flying around.
- After the **"generator"** is down, take out the **engineer**.
- After the engineer is down, keep him down with a couple of **melee dps'ers** on him.

## Generators

**"Generators"** spawn every **~20 seconds**:

- **1** for the first **20%** of the named's health.
- **2** for the next **20%**.
- **3** spawn every ~20 seconds for the rest of the fight.

> If any "generator" is still standing after **15 seconds** of their appearance, the Named will do a **KB** that will send everyone flying all over the place. The named will also **heal himself** a good amount.

## Notes

The fight comes down to having the DPS to take out the generators when they start spawning **3 at a time**.

## DPS Requirements

- DPS on Nexona must be kept **greater than 10k** at all times in order to avoid a nasty AE.
- An additional AoE, **Noxious Rot**, can be avoided if DPS is kept **greater than 25k**.

## Pull & Positioning

- Pull Nexona down the ramp to the **left corner** (left upon entering the room). **MT** group stays down there.
- Assign **4 "shiny" collectors**:
  - One at each weapon rack on either side of the room on the main floor.
  - One outside in the hallway.
  - One up top where Nexona is — three shinies spawn up there: in the middle back, and **BEHIND** each of the two specimens.
- Rest of the raid go up the ramp that is to the left when entering the room and stack on the **"specimen"**.
- **MT** cleric hammer pull or pet pull.

## Specimens

Once Nex is pulled, the entire raid force that is stacked on the **"specimen"** starts killing the specimen. Do this as fast as you can.

- Move to the other side up top where another **"specimen"** is located and take it out fast.
- Run down the ramp on the right side and take out the **"specimen"** there.
- By this time one shiny should have been harvested.

> Harvested shinies spawn an **x2 add**, and they do **memwipes**. Whoever harvested that shiny needs to let the raid know where it (the x2 add) is coming from so they can kill it.

- Leave the **"specimen"** that is in the middle of the lower section — it's of little concern.
- Move to where Nexona is being tanked and take out a **4th "specimen"**.
- The **5th "specimen"** in the middle will be consumed by Nex when she gets to **90%** health — all it does is heal her to 100%. Not worth wasting time on.

## Main Phase

- Focus on the mobs that spawn when someone harvests a shiny, and DPS on Nex in between.

## 45% — Drake Spawn

- When Nex gets to **45%** two **drake 83^^^ heroics** will spawn from the water in the lower section.
- These are easily **mezzed** for the remainder of the fight.
- Drakes should be **mezzed the whole fight** or they will stun and knock back everyone trying to kill shiny adds.
- Having a **healer** that can joust between the shiny group and the mezzers is very helpful in case one breaks.

## Burn

- Concentrate on the shiny-spawned mobs and Nexona.

## Overview

In the same group as [Shade of Khalan Dar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Shade_of_Khalan_Dar).

There are **two methods** of pulling and killing Phara Dar. When you approach Phara Dar's room you will notice the [Shade of Khalan Dar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Shade_of_Khalan_Dar) in the center and a ramp leading up the left side which takes you to Phara Dar. Upon pulling Phara Dar, the Shade of Khalan Dar will immediately fly to wherever Phara Dar is located and assist in the battle. It is imperative to have a **balanced Main and Off Tank group** for this fight as you will need to kill both of these named mobs at once.

## Method 1 — Split at the Ramp

Send two groups (**Main** and an assisting group) up the ramp to Phara Dar and fight the **Shade of Khalan Dar** at the very bottom of the ramp with their back to the wall.

> The drawback: even with snaps and aggro lock, the **Shade of Khalan Dar will still fly up to Phara Dar 2/3 of the time** and have to be dragged back down the ramp to the Off Tank and their assisting group. This can result in a **wipe of the MT group** and a fight reset.

Once the OT group has killed the [Shade of Khalan Dar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Shade_of_Khalan_Dar) at the bottom of the ramp, they should run up and assist in DPS on Phara Dar.

## Method 2 — Hallway Pull (easier)

Pull Phara Dar and subsequently the Shade of Khalan Dar into the hallway that leads to the main room.

- The **MT group** and their assisting party should be located closest to the room entrance.
- The **OT group** and their assisting party should be located approximately **40 metres up the stairs**.
- Positioning **Phara Dar on the bottom far right** and the **Shade of Khalan Dar on the upper far left** corners of the staircase makes it easier for the OT and assisting group to switch over to Phara Dar once the shade is dead.

## Notes

> Please note: The **Shade of Khalan Dar will heal itself to full once during the fight** — this is not a bug.

## Spawn

- Minor **KB**, minor **AoE** if within **20 meters**.
- To spawn him, **click the beehive**.
- **^^ orange heroics** will come — about 4, two sets of them.
- After they are done, the **Queen Bee** will come.
- After she is dead, **Ashenclaw** will spawn.

## Fight

Minor **KB** and minor **AoE** — drag him to one of the hallways and **tank and spank**.

## Pull & Positioning

**MT** can pull this named out of the room, which is recommended. Pull him up the ramp and have the **MT** park against one of the pillars in the upper hallway. He has a pretty hefty AoE on a **1:35 second** timer (seen **55sec - 1min 45sec**). Stand about **50 meters** away (end of the ramp around the corner works well).

Silverwing may need to be pulled into the hallway because of a **healing-to-100%** effect.

## Warding Statues (80%)

***Beginning when Silverwing's health hits 80%***, someone in the raid will be ultra-visioned. It will be announced zonewide: "(target) is now seeing through their mind's eye!"

That person will need to go deactivate the large warding statue by clicking the small device *on the floor next to the large statue*. It can be difficult to see, so be sure to hover over the floor all around the warding statues.

> Until that person deactivates the statue, Silverwing will be ***immune to damage***, but he ***will*** continue to AoE.

### Statue Assignments

- **Fighters** deactivate the first statue on the left when entering Silverwing's room. (SouthWest)
- **Scouts** deactivate the center far side opposite Silverwing. (East)
- **Mages** deactivate the statue opposite the fighters. (Northwest)
- **Priests** deactivate the one where Silverwing resides before he is pulled. (West)

**Note:** If you have not reprogrammed the wards already you will ***not*** be able to deactivate them now.

## Adds (60%)

***Starting at 60% of Silverwing's health,*** adds will start to spawn. A successful strategy is to mesmerize these until Silverwing is Immune, then focus on the adds while the ultra-visioned person works on deactivating their ward statue.

## Levers

There are two **levers** flanking Silverwing's original position. Deactivating one will summon a droag to turn it back on. Put two people on those levers and you will have less problems with the **stuns**.

- If you have finished the quest, [Taking on Trakanon](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Taking_on_Trakanon), he will offer to teleport you to the entrance of [Trakanon's Lair](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Trakanon's_Lair).

## Pull

- Entire raid starts by **facing north**, standing far north of the named near the rocks.
- Have **tank** run up just close enough to bow shot the named, then he needs to run back (get to at least **45 meters** away).
- The entire raid must then run **north** until everyone is **ported** to the little island the named is standing on.

## Jousting Cycle

- After the raid gets ported to the island, they should continue running **north about 30–45 meters** away from the named (back by the rocks).
- **10 seconds** after the port a **massive AoE** goes off. This AoE will only hit people closer than that **30–45 meter** range.
- Once the AoE goes off, the entire raid has about **15 seconds** to run close enough to get some **ranged dps** on the mob.
- After about 15 seconds of dps'ing, everyone needs to start running **north again** until they are at least **45 meters** away from the named.

> It may be helpful to **cancel your current target** so that you don't get turned around when ported, since you might end up running in the wrong direction.

## Snare

> If anyone is still within **45 meters** of the named when he ports you, those people will get a **snare (incurable -75% run speed)**. This means those people that got snared are not going to get far enough away when his massive AoE goes off.

- **Sprint** can be helpful if you are snared.
- If you die, **do not wait to be resurrected** — just revive and start heading north so that when he does his zonewide raid port, you will be brought back instantly and can start fighting again.

## Adds & Enslave

- Continue this jousting until the mob is dead.
- **Adds spawn at 70%, 50% and 30%.**
- At about **50%** the **2nd person in the first group** will be **enslaved** (teleported away from the raid and unable to attack while doing a mining animation).

## Zone-In

- When you enter you are in a zone in/zone out room which leads to gigantic stairs. You must use the side area of the stairs to walk up into the main lair.
- When you first enter the zone, trakanon will greet you from far above the platform, then fly down. At this point you can engage trakanon.

## Requirements

- You must have a **chelsith stone** with you to have any chance to succeed. It will drop in the leviathan encounter in-game.

## The Orb

- Once the fight begins, you will notice the holder of a chelsith stone can touch the **orb altar** on the far side of the platform to energize it.
- Once energized, if you stay within **14m** of the energized orb structure, you are protected from many of trakanon's more powerful abilities.

> The key to winning this encounter is to realize you must all fight within **14m** of the activated orb on the far side of the platform. Trakanon's arcane maelstrom breath weapon is far too lethal outside this protective area. In addition trakanon's knockback abilities will prove problematic, and all but one of them is negated when you are close to this orb object (if it's energized).

## Mechanics

### Wing Buffet & Tail Swipe

- Trakanon is a **level 87 warlock**. He will periodically cast **Wing Buffet** and **Tail Swipe**.
- **Wing Buffet** will **STUN** everyone in front of him for **2.5 seconds**. It will also **KNOCK** everyone back UNLESS they are within **14m** of the energized orb.
- **Tail Swipe** does some damage, and stuns players for about **3 seconds**. It will also **KNOCK BACK** any players that are NOT within **14m** of the energized orb.
- Trakanon is **immune to all control spells**, and will **double attack on melee 10% more** than normal.

### Arcane Aura of the Wyrmlord

- Trakanon has **Arcane Aura of the Wyrmlord** on him.
- On take damage **10% of the time**, players will be hit with **Pestilential Pause**. This does a slight amount of damage to the player and for the next **10 seconds**, all the players spells take much longer to cast, and are basically at **half effectiveness** (heals and damage are half as effective as normal). Also your attack speed is reduced considerably during this time. This effect is **not dispellable**.
- Also, **2% of the time** trakanon is hit with a hostile spell, he will heal himself for **2%**.

### Prophetic Scrutiny

- Trakanon will keep **PROPHETIC SCRUTINY** on **one player** at all times.
- With this effect on you, any spells you cast are interrupted, if the spell is interruptable.
- Every time you start to cast a spell you will be hit with **PROPHETIC BOLT**, which does a small amount of damage and interrupts the spell you are starting to cast.
- This should be active on one player in the raid at all times.

### Prescient Gaze

- Trakanon will keep **Prescient gaze** on **all players** at all times. It should never be off you for more than a few seconds even if you die and are rezzed back in.
- It causes all players to **mana burn**, using health whenever they use mana.
- It also causes players to have **prescient intervention** cast on them **8% of the time** they try and cast a beneficial spell. Prescient intervention interrupts the spell they are trying to cast, and stifles the player for **2 seconds**, and does a small amount of damage to the players mana pool.

### Mark of Repulsion

- One player will generally have **MARK OF REPULSION** on him/her. This spell will go off at some random time, never less than **30 secs** after it was placed on you. It has an **hourglass icon**.
- When it goes off that player will be hit with **static repulsion**, a targeted AOE that WILL knock back all players within **22m** of the person who had Mark of Repulsion on him/her.
- You are **NEVER protected from this knockback**, even if near the orb.

> You must **move away from your raid party** so as not to endanger them with your Mark of Repulsion/Static Repulsion combo.

### Arcane Maelstrom

- **3.5 seconds AFTER** static repulsion goes off, Trakanon will cast **Arcane Maelstrom**.
- A powerful aoe that does **heat damage**, does damage to a random item in everyone's inventory, and does **massive MAGIC BASED health and mana damage** UNLESS you are close to the energized orb.
- You avoid MAGIC BASED MANA AND HEALTH DAMAGE **only if you are near the energized orb**.

### Fetid Cloud & Toxic Tempest

- Trakanon will periodically cast **Fetid Cloud** and **Toxic Tempest** as well.
- **Fetid Cloud** does **disease based** health damage — the closer you are to trak the more disease damage you take, and slows your attack speed.

> If you have **Toxic Tempest** still on you when **fetid cloud** hits you, you will take ADDITIONAL MASSIVE **poison based** health damage.

- **Toxic Tempest** works similarly to Fetid Cloud — does range relative, **poison based** health damage to all players.

> If you have **fetid cloud** on you when **Toxic tempest** hits you, you will take additional MASSIVE **disease damage** as well. Toxic tempest slows your movement rate as well.

### Enrage Timer (Lava)

- **800 seconds** after the raid fight begins, the platform you fight on begins to lower into the lava.
- This takes about **20-30 secs**, after that time you will be forced to fight trakanon in the lava, and it won't likely work well for you.
- If you hop away from the platform, you won't be protected by the orb so that's not really an option.
- If you stay near the orb, you will be covered in lava so that's not really an option.
- You have only about **820 secs** to win this fight.

## Reset

- If trakanon dies, the platform will rise.
- If trakanon resets, the platform will rise.

## Overview

This mob is a relatively simple **tank and spank** encounter.

## Mechanics

- There is a **small arcane AOE** that can be easily avoided by standing at the edge of the room.
- You will also find **floating balls of lightning**. These have a **knockback** and can cause your spells to interrupt.

## Strategy

- The best method of avoiding the lightning balls is to have the **ranged DPS** stand at the very edge of the room.
  - You will notice that the texture of the floor is two different colors — the balls will not affect you if you stand on the **lighter tile**.
- **Healers** move in to attend to tanks and melee DPS as needed.

## Pull

- The **MT** will start by clicking on him — if anyone else does, they will be attacked.

## Mechanics

During the fight he will fall asleep **twice** — there are two different dreams that you must deal with. They can happen in any order, and happen at **80%** and **40%**.

> Anyone underneath Xygoz when he falls asleep will be **instantly killed**.

### First Dream — Mini Dragons

- Spawns miniature versions of all the major dragons in the zone.
- They are all **heroic except for Trakanon**, and not very hard.

### Second Dream — Dolls

- Creates a miniature **doll** of everyone in the raid.
- They spawn at the bottom of the ramp to the **North** (entrance) of the room.
- Everyone must pick up the doll of themselves (**double click**).
- The **last person** to do so will be the one that Xygoz attacks when he wakes up.
- The **MT** does not necessarily have to pick up their doll — they can simply aggro lock Xygoz when he awakens. Leaving the MT's doll up for the remainder of the fight has no negative consequences for the raid.
- Once you have double-clicked your doll, jousting back to the rock will keep the area clear for those having a harder time finding the miniature version of their character.

> Note that this step occasionally **bugs**, and may reset the encounter — just keep trying.

## Other

- Aside from the dreams and an **incurable fear**, there are no special tactics to Xygoz.
- Jousting to the rock will keep the raid from suffering the fear effect.
